ethereum-1x-on-blockchain-interop-and-sc.name: The First Sermon
ethereum-1x-on-blockchain-interop-and-sc.prose: Vitalik stands before the gate of Devcon-Zero in Berlin, the year is 2014, and the Erdtree of Ethereum is a sapling. He speaks of constraints — what cannot be done, what *must* not be done. Listen, for every later land in this map is a footnote to this one talk.
ethereum-1x-on-blockchain-interop-and-sc.rune: the rune of *Constraint*. The protocol's later choices make sense only if you understand what was forbidden at the start.

the-path-to-the-ethereum-light-client.name: The Long Road
the-path-to-the-ethereum-light-client.prose: A talk older than most of the protocol it describes. The light client was promised here. It would not arrive for nine years.
the-path-to-the-ethereum-light-client.rune: the rune of *Patience*. Some debts in this land take a decade to settle.

casper.name: The Whisper of Finality
casper.prose: The first Casper sermon. Vitalik in 2015, gesturing at finality before anyone had a word for it. The proof-of-stake age begins not with the Merge but here, in this whisper.
casper.rune: the rune of *Slashing*. Punishment as a primitive. Every PoS talk descends from this one.

keynote-and-ethereum-roadmap.name: The Map Before the Lands
keynote-and-ethereum-roadmap.prose: Devcon-1, London. Vitalik unfolds a roadmap so dense with foreshadowing that watching it now feels like prophecy. Sharding, Casper, Plasma — names whispered before they had teeth.
keynote-and-ethereum-roadmap.rune: the rune of *Roadmap*. Most of the next decade is annotation upon this drawing.

ethereum-for-dummies.name: The Stranger's Welcome
ethereum-for-dummies.prose: Before the doctrine, the welcome. A gentle hand for the Tarnished who has only just stepped onto the path.
ethereum-for-dummies.rune: the rune of *Account*. State, balance, contract — the three things that make the world.

scalability.name: Layer 2s, Spoken Aloud
scalability.prose: Devcon-1. The word 'L2' before it meant anything. Listen for what was *promised* — fraud proofs, state channels, plasma. Most of these promises broke. The ones that held became Arbitrum, Optimism.
scalability.rune: the rune of *Offload*. Compute elsewhere; settle here.

solidity-for-dummies.name: The First Tongue
solidity-for-dummies.prose: Christian Reitwiessner teaches the language. The dialect has changed; the grammar has not. Read this before you ever read a contract in the wild.
solidity-for-dummies.rune: the rune of *Storage*. Slots, mappings, the cost of forgetting.

smart-contract-security-tips.name: The Old Rules
smart-contract-security-tips.prose: Devcon-2 in Shanghai, weeks before The DAO would be drained. Most of the rules in this talk would be written in blood by 2017. Read them as both gospel and warning.
smart-contract-security-tips.rune: the rune of *Reentrancy*. The first vulnerability the field gave a name.

smart-contract-security.name: The Field Surveys Itself
smart-contract-security.prose: A companion to the Tips. Where the Tips give rules, this maps the threat surface — what an attacker actually does once the contract goes live.

evolving-the-evm-2.name: The Substrate's Confession
evolving-the-evm-2.prose: The EVM speaks of itself in Devcon-2. Why is gas a thing. Why is storage so expensive. Why is the stack so cheap. Listen — every later optimization is in dialogue with these answers.
evolving-the-evm-2.rune: the rune of *Gas*. The metric that governs everything in this land.

the-evm-leaner-meaner-and-closer-to-the-.name: The Inner Workings
the-evm-leaner-meaner-and-closer-to-the-.prose: Pawel Bylica brings you into the engine room of the EVM. Bytecode runs not on stone tablets but on optimized C++. See what is below.
the-evm-leaner-meaner-and-closer-to-the-.rune: the rune of *Interpretation*. The EVM is a contract; the implementation is theater.

live-smart-contract-hacking.name: The Hand on the Wound
live-smart-contract-hacking.prose: You watch someone exploit, live, on stage. The mood is medical, not theatrical. After this talk, you can never read a function the same way again.
live-smart-contract-hacking.rune: the rune of *Adversary*. Your code's first reader is hostile.

breaking-smart-contracts.name: The Bestiary
breaking-smart-contracts.prose: Vulnerability classes, taxonomized. Reentrancy, integer over/underflow, oracle manipulation, signature replay. Each a beast with its own habits.
breaking-smart-contracts.rune: the rune of *Class*. Bugs are not unique; they are members of families.

reversing-ethereum-smart-contracts-to-fi.name: The Bytecode's Mirror
reversing-ethereum-smart-contracts-to-fi.prose: Now read what the compiler emits. Solidity is comfortable; bytecode is the truth. Until you can do this, you cannot fully audit.

evm-some-assembly-required.name: The Inline Tongue
evm-some-assembly-required.prose: Yul. Solidity's assembly. The art of speaking directly to the machine while remaining inside the contract. You will need this for every gas-critical path.

demystifying-smart-contract-security-fac.name: The Reset
demystifying-smart-contract-security-fac.prose: 2024. The field has matured. This talk separates what auditors *say* matters from what *actually* prevents losses. A grim accounting.
demystifying-smart-contract-security-fac.rune: the rune of *Fallacy*. Most security folklore is wrong. Some of it is dangerously wrong.

double-entry-point-issues-from-breaking-.name: The Modern Beast
double-entry-point-issues-from-breaking-.prose: A bug class so subtle it slipped past the best auditors and broke real protocols. Watch this and you have seen the frontier of contract security.
double-entry-point-issues-from-breaking-.rune: the rune of *Double Entry*. Two paths to the same state — one you forgot about.

copying-memory-in-evm-how-hard-can-that-.name: MCOPY's Long Shadow
copying-memory-in-evm-how-hard-can-that-.prose: A 30-minute masterclass on the EVM's memory model, told as the story of a single opcode. Devcon-7. The most pedagogically perfect talk in this entire archive.
copying-memory-in-evm-how-hard-can-that-.rune: the rune of *Memory*. There is no such thing as a simple opcode.

reverse-engineering-evm-bytecode-with-gh.name: The Cartographer's Tools
reverse-engineering-evm-bytecode-with-gh.prose: You learn to map a contract you do not have the source for. Practical RE skills, the kind auditors rely on when only the deployed code is true.

evm-object-format-eof-history-and-motiva.name: The EVM, Reborn
evm-object-format-eof-history-and-motiva.prose: The EVM as it has stood since Frontier is being replaced. EOF is the new format. This is the talk to understand *why*.

liquidity-on-blockchains.name: Pre-DeFi Markets
liquidity-on-blockchains.prose: Devcon-1. Before Uniswap. Before Maker had launched. The framing of what an on-chain market could be, when no one had built one yet.

money-and-debt-and-digital-contracts.name: Rune the Dreamer
money-and-debt-and-digital-contracts.prose: The MakerDAO founder gestures at stablecoins, credit, debt — the entire scaffolding of DeFi as a thought experiment. Pair this with Casper and you have the philosophical core of Ethereum's two great projects.
money-and-debt-and-digital-contracts.rune: the rune of *CDP*. Collateral, debt, position. The cell of every lending protocol since.

building-on-uniswap.name: The Constant Product
building-on-uniswap.prose: Hayden Adams. The mechanism that broke the order book's monopoly on exchange. x*y=k as a primitive of an entire field.

this-is-mev.name: The Dark Forest, Mapped
this-is-mev.prose: Devcon-6. The canonical MEV introduction. After this talk you can never again read a transaction the same way.

mev-for-the-next-billion-its-time-to-get.name: Daian Sharpens the Blade
mev-for-the-next-billion-its-time-to-get.prose: Phil Daian, who first named the beast, returns to argue for PBS, fair ordering, structural fixes. Listen for the resignation in his voice — these problems do not solve themselves.

block-building-after-the-merge.name: The Supply Chain
block-building-after-the-merge.prose: What actually happens between you broadcasting a tx and it landing in a block. The post-Merge pipeline, drawn in detail.

cost-of-feudalism-towards-a-theory-of-me.name: The Theory
cost-of-feudalism-towards-a-theory-of-me.prose: Game-theoretic framing. MEV not as a bug but as the structure of the network's economic gravity.

evaluating-the-pbs-experiment-early-insi.name: The Empirical Verdict
evaluating-the-pbs-experiment-early-insi.prose: Theory meets data. What MEV-Boost did and did not solve, with numbers.

programmable-incentives-intro-to-cryptoe.name: The Economic Foundation
programmable-incentives-intro-to-cryptoe.prose: Devcon-3. Why does this whole construction work at all? Cryptoeconomics, taught from first principles by someone close to the protocol.

empirical-analysis-of-amm-loss-versus-re.name: LVR, Quantified
empirical-analysis-of-amm-loss-versus-re.prose: The most important AMM concept of the last three years, told with measurements rather than handwaves.
empirical-analysis-of-amm-loss-versus-re.rune: the rune of *LVR*. Loss versus rebalancing — the silent tax LPs pay to arbitrageurs.

the-tension-between-mev-and-censorship-r.name: The Endgame Politics
the-tension-between-mev-and-censorship-r.prose: The deepest tension in the protocol's social layer: the same machinery that captures MEV efficiently also makes censorship efficient. There is no clean fix.
the-tension-between-mev-and-censorship-r.rune: the rune of *Tension*. Some problems do not solve; they only shift.

casper-and-consensus.name: The Council
casper-and-consensus.prose: Vitalik, Vlad Zamfir, Emin Gün Sirer, Elaine Shi, sitting on a stage in Cancún. Four of the field's sharpest minds debate finality. Watch this twice.
casper-and-consensus.rune: the rune of *Finality*. There are at least three definitions; they do not commute.

cbc-casper-design-philosophy.name: The Other Casper
cbc-casper-design-philosophy.prose: Vlad Zamfir's branch. Correct-by-construction. The road not taken — but watching it teaches you what the chosen road *isn't*.

the-case-for-proof-of-stake.name: The Argument
the-case-for-proof-of-stake.prose: Why Ethereum committed to PoS. Read this if you have never been convinced the switch was right.

eth-20-tldr.name: The Plan, Compressed
eth-20-tldr.prose: 30 minutes. The whole Eth2 design space, told briefly by someone close to it.

eth2-phase-0-technical-deep-dive.name: The Beacon's Bones
eth2-phase-0-technical-deep-dive.prose: How the beacon chain actually works. Validators, attestations, slots, epochs, justification, finalization. The bones of the post-Merge protocol.

weak-subjectivity-and-eth-20-sync.name: The Subtle Edge
weak-subjectivity-and-eth-20-sync.prose: PoS chains have a property PoW chains do not: you cannot sync from genesis trustlessly. This is *weak subjectivity*. Subtle. Critical.

opening-ceremonies-tim.name: The Heroes of the Merge
opening-ceremonies-tim.prose: Tim Beiko's Devcon-6 retrospective. The Merge has just happened. Listen to the people who shipped it explain what they are still terrified about.

single-slot-finality-and-the-future-of-s.name: Where Consensus Goes
single-slot-finality-and-the-future-of-s.prose: 12 seconds is too long. SSF compresses finality into a single slot. The next great protocol revision lives here.

social-slashing.name: Beyond the Protocol
social-slashing.prose: Some attacks the protocol cannot punish. Social slashing — the layer above the protocol, where coordination decides what is legitimate.

demystifying-libp2p-gossipsub-a-scalable.name: The Gossip Engine
demystifying-libp2p-gossipsub-a-scalable.prose: The pubsub protocol that the beacon chain runs on. Without gossipsub, no consensus.

post-merge-ethereum-client-architecture.name: EL/CL
post-merge-ethereum-client-architecture.prose: The protocol after the Merge: two clients, two networks, one chain. How they speak.

spin-it-up-spin-up-your-own-ethereum-val.name: The Hands-On Vow
spin-it-up-spin-up-your-own-ethereum-val.prose: Stop reading. Run a validator. The fastest path from theory to practice in this entire archive.

solo-staking-in-the-dark-forest-a-surviv.name: The Survival Guide
solo-staking-in-the-dark-forest-a-surviv.prose: What it actually takes to run a validator in 2024 without losing it. Read after you have run one on testnet.

eli5-zero-knowledge.name: The Plain Tongue
eli5-zero-knowledge.prose: Devcon-6. ZK explained without the math. Start here even if you think you do not need to.

zero-knowledge-privacy-and-transparencys.name: The Why
zero-knowledge-privacy-and-transparencys.prose: Why ZK matters at all. The political case before the technical one.

what-to-know-about-zero-knowledge.name: The Survey
what-to-know-about-zero-knowledge.prose: Proof systems compared. STARKs, SNARKs, Bulletproofs, Plonk. Trade-offs laid bare.

stark-from-paper-to-product.name: Eli's Workshop
stark-from-paper-to-product.prose: Eli Ben-Sasson, who proved STARKs into existence, explains how they became products.

proving-evm-bytecode-execution-in-the-zk.name: The Hard Part
proving-evm-bytecode-execution-in-the-zk.prose: How a zkEVM actually works. The opcode-by-opcode proving. Eye-opening if you have never thought about it.

zkevm-vs-evm-full-equivalence.name: The Equivalence Ladder
zkevm-vs-evm-full-equivalence.prose: The canonical Vitalik framing of zkEVM equivalence types. Why some chains call themselves 'Type 1' and others 'Type 4'.

a-better-mental-model-for-rollups-plasma.name: The Reframing
a-better-mental-model-for-rollups-plasma.prose: The single most important talk for understanding L2 security. Watch this even if you do not care about L2s. It will reframe how you think about Ethereum's security as a whole.

demystifying-l2-transactions.name: The Receipt
demystifying-l2-transactions.prose: What actually happens to your tx on an L2. The pipeline from sequencer to data posting to L1 finality.

eli5-account-abstraction.name: The Account Question
eli5-account-abstraction.prose: Why is there an EOA at all? Why must your private key sign every transaction? AA opens these questions.

why-account-abstraction-is-a-game-change.name: The UX Promise
why-account-abstraction-is-a-game-change.prose: Session keys. Social recovery. Sponsored transactions. Multi-call. The features your users will demand once they see them elsewhere.

account-abstraction-on-starknet.name: The Native Path
account-abstraction-on-starknet.prose: Starknet shipped native AA. No 4337. No EntryPoint. Watching this teaches you what 4337 is *working around*.

native-account-abstraction-in-pectra-rol.name: The Convergence
native-account-abstraction-in-pectra-rol.prose: EOF, 7702, 7560 — three EIPs that together end the EOA era. The Pectra-and-beyond endgame.

eip-7702-a-technical-deep-dive.name: The Bridge
eip-7702-a-technical-deep-dive.prose: 7702 lets your EOA temporarily become a smart account. The transition mechanism without which native AA could never deploy.

the-kzg-ceremony-or-how-i-learnt-to-stop.name: The Ceremony
the-kzg-ceremony-or-how-i-learnt-to-stop.prose: What is a trusted setup. What is KZG. Why Ethereum did the largest one ever. Without this you cannot read 4844 or Verkle.

danksharding-workshop.name: The Workshop
danksharding-workshop.prose: EF research's canonical introduction to danksharding, the path from 4844 to full DA scaling.

an-introduction-to-cryptography-new-and-.name: The Foundation
an-introduction-to-cryptography-new-and-.prose: The single best modern cryptography intro in the archive. Watch this *before* anything ZK.

zkps-and-programmable-cryptography.name: The Frontier Frame
zkps-and-programmable-cryptography.prose: ZK as a general programmable primitive. The talk that reframes the whole field as one tool, not a thousand.

defragmenting-ethereum-interoperability-.name: The Fragmentation Problem
defragmenting-ethereum-interoperability-.prose: Twenty L2s. Each its own island. The talk that names what must be solved.

the-verifiability-vision.name: The Verifier's Endgame
the-verifiability-vision.prose: Where the verifiability vision ends: every computation, every chain, verifiable from a phone. The horizon of the field.

keynote-make-ethereum-cypherpunk-again-w.name: The Cypherpunk Reset
keynote-make-ethereum-cypherpunk-again-w.prose: Devcon-7 keynote. The argument that Ethereum has drifted from its origin and must return.

the-history-and-philosophy-of-cypherpunk.name: The Lineage
the-history-and-philosophy-of-cypherpunk.prose: Where cypherpunk came from. Phil Zimmerman, the mailing list, the moral arc that produced Bitcoin and then Ethereum.

satoshi-has-no-clothes-failures-in-on-ch.name: The Naked Ledger
satoshi-has-no-clothes-failures-in-on-ch.prose: Pseudonymity is not privacy. The talk that proves it with on-chain analysis. Required watching for anyone who thinks Ethereum is private by default.

formal-verification-of-smart-contracts.name: The Origin
formal-verification-of-smart-contracts.prose: Devcon-2. The first serious FV talk. The field has grown around this seed.

formal-verification.name: The Council, Again
formal-verification.prose: D3 panel. Daian, Hildenbrandt, Hirai, Luu, Trinkler. The state of the FV field circa 2017, told by its founders.

mud-an-engine-for-on-chain-games.name: MUD
mud-an-engine-for-on-chain-games.prose: Lattice's engine for onchain games. The first credible attempt to make complex state machines feel native to the EVM.

keynote-lessons-learned-from-tor.name: The Tor Mirror
keynote-lessons-learned-from-tor.prose: What a 20-year-old privacy network teaches a 10-year-old one. The lessons are uncomfortable.

towards-a-feature-complete-and-backwards.name: Privacy at L1
towards-a-feature-complete-and-backwards.prose: Privacy not as a separate chain but as a feature of Ethereum itself. The architecture this would require.

bad-proofs-in-formal-verification.name: When the Proof Lies
bad-proofs-in-formal-verification.prose: FV can produce false comfort. This talk shows how. Required after any FV intro.
